name: Vitest
what_it_does: Vite-native unit and integration test framework with Jest-compatible API, HMR-powered watch mode, and first-class ESM and TypeScript support.
use_cases: Generate unit tests for Vite-based projects (Vue, React, Svelte) with zero additional config using vitest init
Drop Jest tests into a Vite project by replacing jest imports with vitest — the API is largely compatible without rewriting tests
Use vi.mock() for module mocking and vi.spyOn() for function spying with the same patterns as Jest but with native ESM support
Run tests in happy-dom or jsdom environments for component testing without a full browser, configurable per test file
Leverage HMR-powered watch mode to get sub-100ms re-run times for changed test files during active development
not_for: Non-Vite projects where Jest is already configured and working — migration provides marginal benefit
Browser E2E testing — Vitest runs in a simulated DOM, not a real browser rendering engine
best_when: Building or migrating a Vite-based application that needs fast unit tests with native TypeScript and ESM support.
avoid_when: Your project does not use Vite and has an established Jest configuration with complex transform pipelines.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error output mirrors Jest's colored diff format; Vitest UI provides a browser-based test result view for visual debugging.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Tests are deterministic when app state is properly reset; vi.clearAllMocks() in afterEach prevents mock state leakage between tests.
known_agent_gotchas: Vitest uses Vite's module resolution — path aliases defined in vite.config.ts are automatically available in tests, but agents must ensure the same config file is used for both app and test runs

vi.mock() is hoisted like jest.mock() but only within Vitest's own transform pipeline; dynamic mock factories that reference outer-scope variables require vi.hoisted() to avoid temporal dead zone errors
The 'browser mode' (Vitest Browser Mode) runs tests in a real browser via WebdriverIO but has a different API from the default jsdom mode; agents must not mix browser-mode and node-mode assertions
Coverage providers (v8 vs istanbul) produce different results for untransformed code; agents configuring coverage must specify the provider explicitly or results will vary by environment
Vitest workspace config allows multi-project setups, but each project must declare its own environment — agents generating monorepo test configs must set environment per project, not globally

security_score_components.security_notes: Secrets should be provided via import.meta.env or process.env; avoid committing .env files with credentials used in test environments.
